Title: Flock of Bleeps
Tagline: You're the only one who showed up.
Description:
The year is somewhere in the far future. Humanity has built a society where robots handle nearly every menial task — but perfection has a cost. Defective or underperforming units are scrapped without ceremony.

You play as one of them. A bumbling, well-meaning, frankly not-very-bright robot judged worthless and dumped in the planet's largest garbage processing zone. You activate face-down in a pile of refuse with memory partially corrupted, one clear directive: get out.

What starts as a simple escape unfolds into something much bigger — a hidden society of discarded machines, four robot factions all pulling at you at once, and eventually a fight for the future of robotkind. None of which you're particularly qualified to lead. But you're the only one who showed up.

CORE PILLARS
- Humor first — absurd NPCs, self-deprecating protagonist, jokes baked into quest design.
- Skill-gated exploration — Metroidvania-style progression where new abilities open old areas.
- Quest variety — fetch-and-return, puzzle-solving, outright sabotage, branching dialog through Offer → Accepted → InProgress → TurnIn → PostQuest stages.
- Faction reputation — every meaningful choice and every NPC death moves karma with four robot factions (Free Robots, Scavengers, R.F.F., Grove). Late-game doors swing on those numbers.
- Branching impact — late-game events reshape based on choices, alliances, and skill investment.
Setting: Far-future Earth — robots run everything; defective ones get scrapped; the discarded built a society in the dump.
Notes:
ACTS

Act I — The Recycling Facility (prologue)
Self-contained tutorial zone. The landfill is enormous, layered, hostile — industrial crushers still operate, mag-cranes sweep overhead, feral scavenger drones patrol the dunes of refuse. Movement / interaction / combat taught here. Terminals scattered throughout deliver worldbuilding and tutorial hints in the voice of long-dead maintenance AIs.

Act II — The Wastes
The surface world. All four faction cities live here, plus the spaces between them. Each region gates one new ability (double jump, wall slide, magnetic grapple, hover dash, etc.) that opens earlier areas. Side quests with hermit robots, confused wildlife, the occasional sympathetic human. Karma per faction tracked from every interaction; faction-locked content opens (or closes) accordingly.

Act III — The Underground
Down. Likely the Grove's deep excavation site — the buried Elder city they've been digging toward — or something adjacent. Old technology, older questions. Faction allegiances from Act II follow you down.

Final boss — TBD.

FACTIONS (current)
- Free Robots — abandoned-village commune. Earnest, mutual, occasionally insufferable. The starting hub in Act II.
- Scavengers — mercenary predators. Will trade with you, work with you, dismantle you for parts the moment the math stops working in your favor.
- R.F.F. (Robots Fight for Freedom) — militant anti-human. Believe coexistence is a lie.
- Inner Sanctum Grove — religious-technical sect studying buried 'Elder' artifacts. Their excavation likely connects to Act III. (Full lore on the Factions page.)
- Robotek — future, lore-only.

SYSTEMS WORTH HIGHLIGHTING (already authored)
- Karma per faction (-50..+100), affected by choices and NPC deaths.
- Contextual death impacts — killing Krang reads differently if you took Eddie's quest vs. unaffiliated. Already wired in NpcData.DeathImpactIdContextual.
- OffendedDialog / OffendedFlag — NPCs can shut you out by behavior.
- Loot tables on NPC death (LootTable + LootEntry → PackedScene).
